metadata.title: Backup Cleanup Feature - Clarifying Questions
metadata.description: This questionnaire helps refine the requirements for the backup file cleanup feature request. Your answers will guide the implementation approach.
metadata.author: Feature Development Team

questions.text: What specific problem are you trying to solve by deleting backup files after questionnaire completion?
questions.description: Understanding the root motivation will help us design the best solution. Select all that apply.

options.label: Disk space concerns - backup files are consuming too much storage
Visual clutter - too many files in the data directory
User confusion - people don't understand what .backup files are for
Compliance/security - need to minimize retained data
Other (please describe in the next question)

questions.text: Please describe the problem you're experiencing in more detail.
questions.description: If you selected 'Other' above, please explain. Otherwise, add any additional context that would be helpful.

questions.text: Do you understand that backup files provide recovery protection if a response file becomes corrupted?
questions.description: This helps us gauge if we need to add educational messaging vs. implementing the feature.

questions.text: If backup files are deleted on completion, would you be comfortable losing the ability to recover a completed response if it becomes corrupted?
questions.description: This is the key trade-off: cleanliness vs. data protection.

options.label: Yes, I'm comfortable with that risk
No, I want to keep recovery capability
I'm not sure - what's the typical failure rate?

questions.text: Are you currently using the default maxBackups setting (5), or have you configured it differently?
questions.description: Enter the number you're using (or 0 if you don't know).

questions.text: If we implement backup cleanup, when should it happen?
questions.description: The timing affects both safety and effectiveness of the cleanup.

options.label: Immediately when questionnaire is marked complete
After the response is exported (JSON/CSV/Markdown)
When the session ends (could be after viewing/exporting)
After a grace period (e.g., 24 hours after completion)
Only when explicitly triggered by user command

questions.text: If we use a delayed cleanup approach, what retention period makes sense?
questions.description: This keeps backups for a safety window, then cleans them up automatically.

options.label: 1 hour (minimal safety window)
24 hours (reasonable safety window)
7 days (conservative approach)
30 days (maximum safety)

questions.text: Which backup files should be cleaned up?
questions.description: The system creates backups for both response files and questionnaire definition files.

options.label: Only response file backups (from incremental completion)
Only questionnaire definition backups
Both response and questionnaire backups
Only backups for completed responses (not abandoned)

questions.text: How should this feature be configured?
questions.description: Determines whether this is automatic, opt-in, or opt-out.

options.label: Default OFF - users enable it if they want it
Default ON - users disable it if they want backups
Always on - no configuration option
Command-line flag per run (e.g., --cleanup-backups)

questions.text: Which alternative approaches would you consider instead of immediate deletion? (Select all that apply)
questions.description: There may be better solutions than the requested approach.

options.label: Reduce maxBackups setting (e.g., from 5 to 2)
Move backups to archive/ subdirectory instead of deleting
Compress backup files to save space
Delete backups older than X days (configurable)
Only delete backups for COMPLETED responses, keep ABANDONED ones

questions.text: If backup cleanup fails during session end, what should happen?
questions.description: This determines how critical we treat cleanup errors.

options.label: Log a warning but continue - cleanup is non-critical
Retry once, then log warning if it fails again
Block session completion - cleanup is required
Queue for background cleanup on next app start

questions.text: Should users be notified when backups are cleaned up?
questions.description: Determines if we add informational messages during cleanup.

options.label: No notification - silent cleanup
Show summary (e.g., 'Cleaned up 3 backup files')
Detailed log of each file deleted

questions.text: Which use cases apply to your questionnaire runs? (Select all that apply)
questions.description: This helps us understand the operational context.

options.label: One-off surveys (run once, never revisit)
Recurring assessments (same questionnaire, multiple runs)
Critical data collection (data loss would be severe)
Experimental/testing use (data is disposable)
Production deployments (multiple users, high volume)

questions.text: How important is this feature to you?
Any additional feedback or requirements we should consider?
questions.description: Open field for any other thoughts on this feature.
